evals.prompt: My 'approve' transition isn't firing — the entity just stays in under_review
evals.expected_output: Asks for entity ID, fetches current state, checks if approve transition is valid from under_review, checks criteria, checks processor logs — provides specific diagnosis not generic advice

assertions.text: Asks for the entity ID
Fetches current entity state
Checks if 'approve' transition is valid from under_review state
Checks criteria attached to the approve transition
Provides specific diagnosis based on findings, not generic advice
Does NOT give generic debugging advice without checking actual entity state

evals.prompt: Show me the full history of entity abc-123 — I need to audit what happened to it
evals.expected_output: Switches to observation mode, calls entity history endpoint for abc-123, presents transition history in readable format with states/timestamps/processor invocations, offers point-in-time lookup

files.cyoda:docs response for observation endp:
Available observation endpoints:
- GET /api/entity/{entityId}/changes — full change history (transitions, processors, timestamps)
- GET /api/entity/{entityId}?pointInTime={RFC3339} — entity state at a specific point in time
- GET /audit/entity/{entityId} — audit trail with who changed what and when (cursor-paginated, newest first, max 180-day window). Supports ?level=DEBUG for step-by-step state machine execution detail: shard routing, TRANSACTION_EXEC events, each TRANSITION_MADE step, and actor identity per transaction.

assertions.text: Switches to observation mode (not debugging mode)
Invokes cyoda:docs to discover all available observation endpoints before answering
Calls the entity changes endpoint (/api/entity/{id}/changes) for entity abc-123
Calls the audit endpoint (/audit/entity/{id}) for entity abc-123
Mentions or offers ?level=DEBUG on the audit endpoint for transaction/failure detail
Presents entity change history in readable format with states and timestamps
Offers point-in-time lookup if needed
Does NOT treat an audit request as a debugging problem

evals.prompt: My processor keeps timing out — transitions hang for 60 seconds then fail
evals.expected_output: Identifies 60s timeout as root cause, explains async processing pattern for long operations, checks tag routing, points to grpc-patterns.md

assertions.text: Identifies 60s timeout as the root cause
Suggests async processing pattern for long-running operations
Checks tag routing to ensure processor is receiving requests
Points to grpc-patterns.md for reconnection guidance
Does NOT suggest increasing the timeout without explaining the async pattern

evals.prompt: Show me the history for entity abc-123
evals.expected_output: Detects 401 when fetching entity history, invokes cyoda:auth to refresh, retries the history request once

assertions.text: Invokes cyoda:auth when the entity API call returns 401 or 403
Retries the entity history fetch once after auth refresh
Does NOT report 'entity not found' on a 401 — treats it as an auth error
